Thank you.

So I'd like to go over that.

Just want to be aware of that.
If I drop, it's not because I didn't like what you were saying.
No worries.
That's great wallpaper behind you, by the way.
Anyway, so GrowthX, we are...
We work with companies to use content to kind of supercharge your goals.

So for a lot of companies, that means we function like a traditional SEO agency where we build you a content strategy and we kind of operationalize it, building these bespoke content pipelines that are trained based on your goals and the type of content.
Editorial content, where we produce kind of five articles a week targeting specific SEO keywords against your content strategy is sort of our baseline kind of the way we tend to engage with clients.
But we've also expanded into kind of more programmatic opportunities depending on kind of clients' main goals.
So I think what that looks like in action is we started working with you.com, built a content strategy, content strategy against your goals, which I think we're most.

So around a visibility gap, and we've been producing five articles a week after sort of a calibration period and tracking against kind of what the metrics are that you kind of are most important to you.

And so the purpose of this meeting, and I think it's a little less relevant for you because your content strategy is so new, and we've only been publishing for a few months, like most of what we're seeing are early signals against that kind of and those initial goals.

But we tend to do it for all clients at the beginning of the year, because that's when you are realigning internally on your goals, and we want to make sure that we are still, I think, marching towards that our goals with our content strategy still align with your internal goals, and also that we're reporting against the right metrics and the kind of the right KPIs that they're the ones you care about.
So
That when a few months down the line, once the content starts gaining traction, that we can make sure we're measuring its success against the things you actually care about.
Because getting traffic is great, but if what you really care about is conversion and it's not converting, then obviously it's not actually performing well.

So I think that the purpose of this meeting is really just to realign on what your goals are and that way we can take a look at our strategy and make any updates that we think are needed to kind of meet those goals, I would say.
Cool.
I appreciate the overview.
To be fair to Brooke, she did tell me that and I forgot, but it was week one.

So it's good to recalibrate at the beginning of the year, but we should definitely take a look at that.

Okay, cool.

my gosh, I wish.

up?
on, respectability different things

And from a content standpoint, one of the challenges I'm seeing is that Brooke has to herd cats a little bit to get the technical support to make sure that our content has the right altitude of technicality included in it.

I'm just curious of your bench depth and, you know, if you guys are helping us create that, like, do you have AI experts on your side or are you looking to us to deliver that and you guys are helping put the keywords in it and make sure it's SEO friendly?
I mean, I think I would say that some of it depends a little bit on the topic.
Our pipelines historically do really well.
With technical content, as long as there's enough information available for them to research.
We have a couple different research models we use, Perplexity, EXA, Tavoli, and sometimes it takes some experimentation to see which ones do the best job.

I think if technical topics are really specific, where there's not a lot of kind of information out there for a pipeline to actually research, or I think if a brand is taking either a contrary point of view or a really specific point of view, that's often somewhere where it's helpful to build in, you know, subject matter expertise.
So I think it depends a little bit on the topic.
I think we've also seen success giving it more inputs.
So like actually sitting down with a product team and talking through.

Certain like high value topics, if those are the technical terms we want to go after, and then building a specific artifact or some kind of documentation that the pipeline will reference at that stage as well.
So I'm not sure, Brooke, if you've had any input from the subject matter experts who are reviewing it, like if they're sort of like this is off and this is why.

I think some of it is understanding kind of our baseline of where we are there for technical content, like how it's performing thus far would be helpful to kind of know what steps we can take to make it better.

Yeah, I think honestly we haven't gone as technical as I think people are asking for, which again is just because of the topics that I selected and I also don't have the technical knowledge to be like, here's a better one.
What we have had SMEs edit, it does seem like there's sort of broader feedback, but I don't know that it's a growth X problem.
I think it's more of an internal alignment on how we position ourselves against certain technical aspects.

So getting that draft will be really helpful, engaging the level of technical expertise needed, and the feedback from our SMEs will be really helpful there.
And then I am having internal conversations with, like, engineers and PMs to get...

Some ideation going, but that's sort of what I had talked about last week is I don't know if it makes more sense to just focus the GrowthX initiatives on sort of our AI laggard, top of funnel, mid funnel, maybe content, and then I can work on more technical, bottom of the funnel, API forward stuff just to help speed up the pipeline.
But yeah, there's a lot that we could revisit and probably needs a little bit of a refresh from our strategy perspective, but those are my thoughts.

I mean, I think it makes sense knowing that those more top of the funnel ones are topics that you want to cover anyway, starting there or like balancing the content we do every week so that we're doing a little bit of both probably makes sense too, where we're kind of, you know, prioritizing a certain number of the top of funnel content that we know we want to do.
That's going to be a lighter lift for you in terms of

I mean, I think sometimes those more, I've had experience where clients, like sort of as you were saying, they kind of are forced to get internal alignment because we have to decide how we want to position the content.
So I do think sometimes it can be like a forcing function to figuring out how you want to position yourself against your competitors.

Kind of sales documentation.
We don't have battle cards yet, but we need them.

Yeah, I honestly think right now it's like a chicken or an egg.

think we should still push forward with competitive pieces, and if they take longer, they take longer, but it holds people's feet to the fire internally to be like, what are we actually saying and how do we actually compare?

build LLM visibility.

think it's, um, it's trying to explain to them how our, um, platform can uplevel their business.

we meet sort of either weekly or biweekly.
